{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why: it is both part of the cycle and the only reported layer violation:

```text
src/application/billing-flow.ts
 -> src/domain/billing.ts
 -> src/presentation/invoice-view.ts
 -> src/application/billing-flow.ts
```

The domain layer should not depend on presentation. Keeping that edge means billing domain logic has a runtime dependency on invoice UI code, which is the most likely cause of initialization/re-entry problems in the circular dependency.

Best fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral module, or invert the dependency.

Recommended shape:

```text
src/domain/billing.ts
  imports only domain-owned types/helpers

src/presentation/invoice-view.ts
  imports from src/domain/billing.ts

src/application/billing-flow.ts
  coordinates billing + invoice rendering
```

If the domain import is only for TypeScript types, convert it to `import type`. If it needs runtime behavior, extract a domain-owned interface or DTO, then let presentation implement/render it instead of being called by the domain."}}
